methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples for palynomorph concentration estimation and morphological analysis.

steps.description: Each sample was dried and weighed.

A total of 10 g of sediment was treated with room temperature 7.5% HCl for several hours, and room temperature 38% HF for ~2 days with thrice-daily swirling, and neutralization steps using distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: treat sediment with HCl and HF, neutralize with distilled water

steps.description: One tablet of Lycopodium clavatum spores was added to the organic residue, which was vigorously mixed and then sieved through a 10-μm nylon mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium spores, mix, and sieve through 10-μm mesh

steps.description: Sieved residues were stained with safranin-o and mounted on microscope slides using glycerine jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: stain residues with safranin-o and mount on slides

steps.description: At least two slides were made from the > 10 μm sieved residue of each sample for counting under the microscope.
The residues from selected samples were also sieved at 20 μm and microscope slides made for morphologic/taxonomic analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve residues at 20 μm and prepare slides for analysis

steps.description: Surplus residues from samples TB2-22, TB2-00, TB2-97 were heated to ~80°C in a 3% wt. solution of sodium hexametaphosphate for 1 h, ultrasonicated for 1 min, and sieved at 10 μm and 20 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: heat residues, ultrasonicate, and sieve for concentration

steps.description: All surplus residues were stored in vials containing glycerine to which one drop of liquefied phenol had been added to prevent microbial growth.
steps.searchable_summary: store residues in glycerine with phenol
